svg.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif
svg.title: Queen MQ vs Kafka: head-of-line blocking at high entity cardinality
svg.desc: Same 12 entities pushed into Kafka (left) and Queen MQ (right). Kafka hash-mods entities into 4 fixed partitions, so 3 unrelated entities share each partition. When one entity goes slow, the other two sharing its partition are blocked. Queen gives every entity its own logical lane, so a slow entity stalls only itself.

g.text: user:007 and user:011 share p2 with the slow user:003. They have nothing to do with each other.
user:003 has its own lane. Its 28-second tool call stalls only itself.
